title: Rapid Ransomware Recovery & Architecture Overhaul for Financial Services
clientContext: A regional bank with $5B+ in assets was targeted by a sophisticated Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) syndicate that successfully encrypted core customer databases and paralyzed cross-branch operations.
challenge: The bank faced complete operational paralysis, severe reputational damage, and imminent regulatory fines. They required immediate containment without destroying forensic evidence, followed by a secure rebuild.
approach: Our Digital Forensics and Incident Response (DFIR) team isolated the infected network segments within 2 hours. We conducted a forensic analysis to identify the entry point (a compromised VPN credential) and safely restored 100% of critical systems from immutable offline backups without paying the ransom.
solution: Post-recovery, we eradicated the remaining malware, deployed advanced EDR (CrowdStrike), and completely rebuilt the network architecture using Zero Trust principles and micro-segmentation.
whatWeLearned: Immutable backups are the ultimate fail-safe against modern ransomware. Relying solely on perimeter defenses is a guaranteed path to failure in the financial sector.

title: Achieving Dual SOC2 & HIPAA Compliance for a Hyper-Growth HealthTech
clientContext: A fast-growing telemedicine platform needed to prove their security posture to secure highly lucrative, multi-year enterprise contracts with national hospital networks.
challenge: The startup lacked formal security policies, had fragmented access controls across AWS and Google Cloud, and possessed no clear roadmap or bandwidth to achieve SOC2 Type II or HIPAA compliance before their contract deadlines.
approach: We conducted a comprehensive gap analysis against both frameworks. We then built a prioritized, automated remediation roadmap, focusing on high-risk technical controls before drafting the administrative policies.
solution: We implemented strict Role-Based Access Control (RBAC), enforced end-to-end encryption for all PHI (data at rest and in transit), and deployed compliance automation tooling to continuously gather audit evidence.
whatWeLearned: Automating compliance evidence collection from day one transforms audits from a multi-month nightmare into a week-long formality.

title: Securing Global E-Commerce Infrastructure Before Black Friday
clientContext: A multinational retailer expecting record-breaking traffic during the Q4 holiday season launched a newly refactored, microservices-based web platform.
challenge: The client needed absolute assurance that their new architecture could withstand highly targeted, financially motivated cyber attacks during their most critical revenue window.
approach: We executed an aggressive, intelligence-led grey-box Web Application Penetration Test. Our red team emulated the tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs) of known e-commerce threat actors (e.g., Magecart).
solution: We discovered and successfully exploited critical SQL injection flaws in the legacy database connector and a broken authentication flow in the new API gateway. We worked directly with their DevSecOps team to patch and verify the fixes prior to launch.
whatWeLearned: Even modern, scalable microservices architectures can be critically vulnerable if legacy integrations and custom authentication logic are not rigorously tested.

title: Automated Cloud Security Posture Management for Global SaaS
clientContext: A B2B enterprise software company was scaling rapidly across hundreds of AWS accounts, but struggling with severe misconfigurations and uncontrolled IAM sprawl.
challenge: Overly permissive IAM roles (wildcard privileges) and publicly exposed S3 buckets were creating massive security risks. The DevOps team was deploying faster than the security team could audit.
approach: We conducted a comprehensive audit of the entire AWS Organization. Rather than slowing down deployments, we implemented a 'secure-by-default' least-privilege access model using Infrastructure as Code (IaC) guardrails.
solution: We deployed industry-leading Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) tools and automated remediation scripts for common misconfigurations (e.g., automatically closing public S3 buckets and revoking unused IAM keys).
whatWeLearned: In the cloud, infrastructure is code. Security must be embedded directly into the CI/CD pipeline (Shift-Left) rather than treated as a post-deployment audit.

title: 24/7 Threat Detection for a Converged IT/OT Manufacturer
clientContext: A tier-1 automotive manufacturing firm operated a complex, globally distributed environment spanning traditional IT systems and sensitive Operational Technology (OT) on the factory floor.
challenge: The internal security team operated only 9-to-5 and lacked the visibility and expertise to monitor the OT network. They were highly vulnerable to lateral movement from IT into the factory floor.
solution: We onboarded the client to our Managed Detection and Response (MDR) platform, providing 24/7/365 "eyes on glass" monitoring, proactive threat hunting, and automated threat containment.
approach: We integrated their existing firewalls and endpoint agents into our SIEM, deployed specialized OT network sensors (Claroty) on the factory floor, and established AI-driven behavioral baselines to detect anomalies.
whatWeLearned: Bridging the airgap between IT and OT security requires specialized sensor deployment and a deep understanding of industrial control system protocols.
